9:
Such people are proud and stubborn, often to their own detriment. They lack flexibility and willingness to yield. They cannot allow themselves to look foolish in the eyes of others, nor can they adapt their behavior gracefully to the situation. They tend to analyze their actions after the deed is done rather than before.
 They do not know what they truly want, and they search for meaning in everything. They may apply their knowledge in ways that do not fit the situation, and the result is "too clever for one's own good." Very often they pursue other people's goals rather than their own, and in the end they are left with nothing. 
Since they do not always understand the purpose behind their actions, they may act chaotically and become overly absorbed in the process itself. Because they do not know what they want, they are easily led astray. They find it hard to see things through to the end; they cannot perceive the final goal, and they may abandon a task just a few steps short of the result. 
They are guileless; they do not know how to pretend, scheme, or persuade. Money is not the main thing in life for them — emotions and relationships with people matter more. They do not know how to ask for things; it is easier for them to insist by asserting power, to demand, or to compel.
They are very proud. To ask is, for them, a humiliation. If they are refused once, they will not ask a second time. Their pride will not allow them to lower themselves again. Moreover, if after a refusal they do eventually receive what they wanted, they take no pleasure in it, perceiving it as though a favor were being done for them.
 They do not know how to take for themselves. They are characteristically selective about what to accept and what to decline. The form in which an offer is made matters greatly to them — if it does not sound respectful enough in their view, they will not accept it. Because of this, many opportunities slip through their fingers. They are also inclined to refuse any offer at first and then regret it later. 
All their lives they feel underappreciated, and from this arise grievances toward those around them. 
Their psychological difficulty lies in the fact that they have no goals of their own and therefore often help others achieve theirs. As a result they are left with nothing, since the goal belonged to the person they were helping — and so, naturally, did the fruit of that goal.
99:
Two nines in the Psychomatrix indicate the presence of a strong memory, one that allows a person to recall the needed information quickly and without loss. If, however, a person bearing such a sign is forgetful and complains of a weak memory, the reasons for this discrepancy must be examined. One of the most common causes of weakened memory is when, instead of developing their mental faculties, a person burdens themselves with all sorts of small problems and obligations (everyday drudgery) — this applies especially to mothers on maternity leave. 
This sector also grants a rich vocabulary, beautiful and abundant speech. Good linguistic abilities — a person can master a language within a few months. A good sense of humor.
They adapt quickly to a new environment, do not lose their bearings in a new place, and can orient themselves rapidly. 
Born drivers. Strong memory. A gift for languages — with a 6 present, a person may become a translator. The linguist's code: logical thinking, the capacity to handle large volumes of information. 
They may have vivid and prophetic dreams, experience visions or borderline states (in such cases dreams are a hint from the Universe or from the person's own subconscious, so they should interpret their dreams themselves, without dream books). They possess powerful intuition and intellect. But there is one enormous drawback — touchiness; such people can hold and accumulate grievances for a long time, replaying situations and refusing to let them go. 
Recommendations: let go of grievances, talk things through often.
999 — psychics, but these people can lose their minds from their own visions. They often display arrogance and thereby complicate their karma. 9999 — too clever for their own good. They constantly need intellectual activity. A risk of schizophrenia; excellent students and teachers. 
99999 — geniuses, often bordering on schizophrenia. If they have the first blood type (the aristocratic type), they often turn to drink — and likewise with 555; caution is advised, especially for women. The 358 code can also fall into drinking. 
When there are many 9s, such people are always misunderstood. Their mind and the mind of the average person are forever on different planes. "Too clever for one's own good" is exactly about them. 
111 and more, 99 and more — if such a person leads, there is always progress; they are far-sighted. When they speak, they pace constantly back and forth (they cannot stand in one place). Illness — stomach ulcers (a predisposition).
999:
Of such people one may say: "I want everything, all at once." They are torn apart by their desires. 
These are diplomats by nature; they are able to be cunning and to adapt to the situation in order to achieve their aim. They know how to negotiate and to build bridges between people. They know not only how to ask but how to coax and wheedle. A refusal does not upset them; they get their way by various means — "if not by washing, then by rolling." They will keep persuading until they get what they want. No one can talk someone into doing something quite like they can. They are magnificent salespeople. 
They are very pleasant in conversation, eager to please everyone, to come across as good to all. This striving to please both sides may lead to both sides regarding them as traitors.
They live life at high speed; they possess great flexibility and a heightened drive toward their goal. They try to bring down every hare with a single shot, they always keep a long list of desires, they bustle about a great deal, and this bustle scatters them among trifles — the main thing gets lost. Life as a process loses its meaning. It turns into a constant race.
They are prone to sudden shifts of mood and can quickly arrive at disappointment, for upon reaching a goal they lose interest in it and rush off to the next one without taking time to savor what they have achieved. Periods of extraordinary activity give way to depression until a new goal appears toward which they must run. Because of this there is a constant turnover of events; their life turns into an unbroken race, and reaching a goal does not make them happy. Behind the goal they do not see the price that must be paid for it, and so the means often turn out to be more costly than the result itself. All of this leads to the burning up of their own energy. 
Their psychological difficulty is depression.
To harmonize their psychological and physical state:
Value and enjoy what already is, what has already been achieved. This may include not only things but also people, information, health, and so on. Learn to take pleasure in it.
While moving toward something, track and note the pluses and minuses. If the minuses outweigh the pluses, give up that goal. This will help you understand what is truly useful and necessary for you, and it will conserve energy for the achievement of important goals.
9999:
Of them one may say: "The goal at any price." When they need something, the ground burns beneath their feet. They are capable of pushing toward a goal head-on, heedless of any obstacle and using every available means. On the path to a goal they are nearly impossible to stop. 
Periods of extraordinary activity give way to apathy, disappointment, and depression until a new goal arises. Because of this there is a constant turnover of events; their life turns into an unbroken race, and reaching a goal does not make them happy. Behind the goal they do not see the price that must be paid for it, and so the means often turn out to be more costly than the result itself. All of this leads to the burning up of their own energy.
Their psychological difficulty is depression.

нет:
Such people are distinguished by a weakened memory; they may be absent-minded and inattentive. They find it hard to adapt to a new environment or to a group, and they can easily lose their way in unfamiliar territory. Difficulties with learning a foreign language. Difficulties with driving a car. 
The upside of this sector is the absence of touchiness. 0–9: a weak memory that needs strengthening — if a person accumulates grievances, they become forgetful in their work. 
Recommendations: it is essential to train the memory, especially in children — languages, poems.
